beats.speakable_text: If a fast sure, no problem is familiar, this boundary pattern needs a better reset.
Trying to outrun boundaries usually makes the loop more expensive. More over-committing, more silent resentment. And less energy for what you actually chose.

The boundary struggle often persists because saying no gets processed as a risk to the relationship instead of routine maintenance of it. Pushing harder can look like progress while the underlying pattern stays the same.
Try this. Name what you can actually offer, say it in one plain sentence. And stop there without the apology tour. Keep it small enough to finish before your brain starts negotiating.
Bookmark the pattern. Signal, reset, next action. A clear no now costs less than a resentful yes later.
